### Post by sh1 on 2009-06-02
Hi all,

I have a Dell 1501 with ATI 1150 Express and I have problems with Xorg configuration in FreeBSD 7.2 , today I tried latest version of Ubuntu whitout any problem regarding Xorg server configuration...after boot the resolution is 1280x800 without touching the config file.
I was curious to see what driver is used and how it is configured because I want to try same settings in FreeBSD, but when I opened the file and I saw those lines in xorg.conf :

```

# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.

```Does anybody tell me where are Xorg settings located and which driver is used ?

Thanks.

---

### Post by Legace on 2009-06-02
You are using either the radeon (most likely) or radeonhd drivers.
- Open Synaptic packange manager, search for **radeon** and you'll see which one is installed :)

---

### Post by sica07 on 2009-06-02
I have the same model and I run archlinux on it. I used the open source xf86-video-ati diver. For more details read: [http://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/Beginners_Guide#ATI_Graphics_Cards](http://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/Beginners_Guide#ATI_Graphics_Cards). I hope it helps. ;)

---
